description_long: This object is a double-belled trumpet with a striking, industrial design, featuring two large, flared brass bells at one end and a complex arrangement of tubing, valves, and mechanical components. The body is predominantly metallic with a glossy, reflective finish, and it includes three piston valves positioned centrally, each topped with a round button. The instrument is adorned with additional mechanical elements, such as a mesh-covered chamber and various cylindrical and rectangular attachments, giving it a steampunk-inspired appearance. The handle and mouthpiece area are robust, with visible bracing and a dark, possibly rubberized grip. The leadpipe curves gently from the mouthpiece to the valve block, and the main tuning slide is U-shaped, located just before the bells. The overall construction suggests a custom or experimental trumpet, blending traditional brass instrument features with modern or fantastical embellishments.
description: A double-belled, glossy metallic trumpet with intricate tubing, three central piston valves, and steampunk-inspired mechanical details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two large, flared bells side by side, emphasizing the instrument's dual-bell design.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the trumpet, the arrangement of valves, tubing, and mechanical attachments.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the handle, mouthpiece area, and the symmetrical alignment of the bells.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the valves, tubing, and additional mechanical features from the other side.
attribute_bell_flare: Wide, abrupt flare with a pronounced, circular opening at each bell.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three piston valves arranged in a straight line along the top center of the instrument.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: Gently curved leadpipe with a smooth transition from the mouthpiece to the valve block.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: U-shaped main tuning slide located just before the bells, oriented horizontally and connected to the leadpipe and valve section.
